# HOA Agent
> Free public database of HOA and condo community information for Palm Beach County, Florida. Lookup fees, restrictions, management companies, litigation history, news reputation, and resident reviews for over 8,000 communities.

## Core pages
- [Home](https://www.hoa-agent.com): Search and discover Palm Beach County HOA communities
- [About](https://www.hoa-agent.com/about): What HOA Agent is and why it exists
- [Search](https://www.hoa-agent.com/search): Find any HOA or condo by name, address, or city
- [Cities](https://www.hoa-agent.com/city): Browse communities by city in Palm Beach County
- [For Agents](https://www.hoa-agent.com/for-agents): Real estate professional resources
- [Pricing](https://www.hoa-agent.com/pricing): Free tier and paid report details
- [HOA Fee Report 2026](https://www.hoa-agent.com/reports/hoa-fee-report-2026): Palm Beach County HOA fee data and trends
- [Guides](https://www.hoa-agent.com/guides): How-to guides on Florida HOAs and condos
- [Florida HOA Law](https://www.hoa-agent.com/florida-hoa-law): Plain-English explainers of Chapters 718 and 720
- [Methodology](https://www.hoa-agent.com/methodology): How we collect and verify data

## What we cover
For each HOA and condo community:
- Monthly fee range (min, median, max)
- Management company name and contact
- Litigation history from CourtListener
- News reputation score (1-10)
- Special assessment signals
- Resident reviews and comments
- Florida entity status and registration data
- Unit count and property type
- Pet restrictions, rental restrictions, STR rules

## Data sources
- Florida Division of Corporations (Sunbiz)
- Palm Beach County Property Appraiser (PBCPAO)
- Palm Beach County Clerk of Courts
- CourtListener federal and state legal databases
- NewsAPI and Guardian API
- Resident submissions (verified by admin)

## Coverage
- Palm Beach County, Florida (current — 8,000+ communities)
- Broward and Miami-Dade counties (planned 2026)
- All 67 Florida counties (planned 2027)

## Key terms
Special assessment: A one-time fee charged to HOA members for unexpected expenses like roof repairs, pool resurfacing, or building envelope work. Florida law requires advance notice and vote for most assessments.

Master HOA: A top-level association governing an entire planned development. Sub-associations exist within it governing specific neighborhoods or buildings.

News reputation score: HOA Agent scores each community 1-10 based on matched news coverage. 1-3 = High Risk, 4-5 = Under Scrutiny, 6-7 = Mixed, 8-9 = Good Standing, 10 = Excellent.

Chapter 718 vs Chapter 720: Florida Statute Chapter 718 governs condominiums. Chapter 720 governs homeowners associations. They have different rules for assessments, board elections, reserves, and owner rights.
